about.title: About Us | CS-Arena
about.description: Learn more about CS-Arena, the ultimate platform for computer science students.
about.hero_subtitle: Empowering the next generation of software engineers to build, collaborate, and get hired. We turn hidden local projects into global portfolios.
about.mission_desc: CS-Arena was born out of a simple observation: Computer Science students build incredible graduation and side projects, but these masterpieces often end up hidden in private repositories or forgotten on local hard drives. Our mission is to provide a dedicated, professional stage where your code can shine, connecting brilliant minds with real-world opportunities.
about.feature1_desc: Present your tech stack, GitHub repo, and architecture in a sleek unified dashboard.
about.feature2_desc: Through our upvote systems, the best community projects organically rise to the top.
about.feature3_desc: Connect with developers who want to contribute to meaningful open-source projects.
about.tech_desc: We understand the modern tech landscape. CS-Arena is a highly optimized Full-Stack application powered by <bold>Next.js 15 (App Router)</bold>, leveraging <bold>React 19</bold> features. We utilize <bold>Sanity CMS</bold> for robust content management and integrate directly with live GitHub APIs. Whether you are a MERN stack wizard or a systems programmer, this is your arena.
about.cta_desc: Don't let your hard work collect virtual dust. Submit your project today and let the world see what you've built.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy | CS-Arena
privacy.description: Learn how we collect, use, and protect your data at CS-Arena.
privacy.intro: At CS-Arena, we believe in open-source engineering and transparent data practices. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you interact with our platform. We only ask for personal information when we truly need it to provide a service to you.
privacy.section1_intro: To provide a seamless developer experience, we collect minimal data required for authentication and project showcasing:
privacy.section1_list1: Authentication Data: When you log in using GitHub OAuth via NextAuth.js, we securely receive your public profile information (Name, Username, Email address, and Avatar). We do not have access to your GitHub passwords.
privacy.section1_list2: Project Submissions: Information you voluntarily provide when submitting to the arena, such as repository URLs, tech stacks, video demos, and project descriptions.
privacy.section1_list3: Interaction Data: We store basic interaction metrics (like upvotes and views) to maintain the integrity of our Global Leaderboard.
privacy.section2_intro: Your data is exclusively used to power the CS-Arena platform and enhance community collaboration. We use it to:
privacy.section2_list1: Create and manage your public developer profile.
privacy.section2_list2: Fetch and display live statistics (Stars, Forks, Issues) directly from public GitHub APIs to your project cards.
privacy.section2_list3: Prevent spam and manipulation of the upvoting system.
privacy.section2_list4: Connect you with potential open-source contributors or recruiters.
privacy.section2_outro: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third-party marketers or advertisers.
privacy.section3_intro1: We build on top of modern, secure infrastructure. Your project details and profile information are stored securely in Sanity's Content Lake. Authentication sessions are handled via cryptographically secure, HTTP-only cookies managed by NextAuth.js.
privacy.section3_intro2: While we implement industry-standard security measures, no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure. We continuously monitor our systems to ensure your data remains protected.
privacy.section4_intro: You have full ownership of your data. At any time, you can:
privacy.section4_list1: Edit or update your project submissions.
privacy.section4_list2: Unlink your GitHub account from the platform.
privacy.section4_list3: Request the complete deletion of your profile and associated projects from our database by contacting the maintainers.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy | CS-Arena
cookies.description: Transparency on how CS-Arena uses cookies and local storage.
cookies.intro: At CS-Arena, we believe in complete transparency about what we store in your browser. Unlike many modern platforms, we keep our storage footprint incredibly light. This page explains our use of cookies and local storage.
cookies.section1_intro: CS-Arena uses essential cookies strictly for security and authentication purposes. We do not use cookies for tracking or advertising.
cookies.section1_list1: NextAuth Session: When you log in via GitHub, a secure, HTTP-only session cookie is created by NextAuth.js. This cookie is strictly necessary to keep you logged in as you navigate the platform and securely submit projects.
cookies.section1_list2: CSRF Token: A token used to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks, ensuring your interactions with our server are secure.
cookies.section2_intro: To provide a blazing-fast user experience and prevent server overload, we utilize your browser's Local Storage for non-sensitive UI state management:
cookies.section2_list1: Upvote Persistence: We store keys like upvote_[project_id] and interacted_[project_id] locally. This helps us optimistically update the UI when you like a project, ensuring a seamless experience while preventing accidental spam on our Leaderboard APIs.
cookies.section2_list2: Theme Preferences: If applicable, we may store your preference for Light or Dark mode to prevent screen flickering during page navigation.
cookies.section3_intro: We respect your privacy as a developer. Therefore, CS-Arena does not use:
cookies.section3_list1: Google Analytics or intrusive user-flow tracking scripts.
cookies.section3_list2: Facebook/Meta Pixels or any social media advertising trackers.
cookies.section3_outro: Your activity on our platform is yours alone, and it is never sold to advertisers.

terms.title: Terms of Service | CS-Arena
terms.description: The rules, guidelines, and agreements for using the CS-Arena platform.
terms.intro: Welcome to CS-Arena. By accessing or using our platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully before submitting your projects or interacting with the community. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you are prohibited from using the platform.
terms.section1_desc: By connecting your GitHub account and using CS-Arena, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms of Service. These terms apply to all visitors, users, and others who access the service.
terms.section2_intro: CS-Arena is a professional stage for your code. You are solely responsible for the projects, links, and content you submit. By using the platform, you agree to the following code of conduct:
terms.section2_list1: No Malicious Content: You will not submit repositories containing malware, viruses, or any code designed to harm or exploit other users.
terms.section2_list2: Respect Intellectual Property: You must own the rights to the code you submit or have explicit permission to share it. Do not pass off others' work as your own.
terms.section2_list3: Accurate Representation: Project descriptions, tech stacks, and team member details must be accurate and truthful.
terms.section2_outro: CS-Arena reserves the right to remove any project or ban any user that violates these guidelines without prior notice.
terms.section3_desc1: You retain full ownership of your code and projects. CS-Arena does not claim any intellectual property rights over the repositories you link.
terms.section3_desc2: However, by submitting your project to CS-Arena, you grant us a worldwide, royalty-free, and non-exclusive license to display your project details, pitch, thumbnail images, and GitHub statistics on our platform for promotional and community-building purposes.
terms.section4_desc: CS-Arena is provided on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis. While we strive to provide a seamless experience, we make no warranties, expressed or implied, regarding the platform's uptime, accuracy of GitHub statistics, or the success of finding contributors through our service.
